titleStmt.title: Mr. Alexander C. Kirk, United States Political Adviser on the Staff of the Supreme Allied Commander, Mediterranean Theater ( (Alexander),), to the Secretary of State

div.head: Mr. Alexander C. Kirk, United States Political Adviser on the Staff of the Supreme Allied Commander, Mediterranean Theater ( Alexander ),Mr. Kirk was also American Ambassador in Italy and representative on the Advisory Council for Italy. to the Secretary of State
div.p:
1228. Our No. 1206, March 28, 6 p.m.Not printed. We learned this afternoon that Governor Lehman telegraphed HendricksonRoy F. Hendrickson, Deputy Director General of UNRRA. yesterday stating that UNRRA Central Committee had approved an approach to HoxhaEnver Hoxha, Prime Minister of the Provisional Albanian Government and Commander in Chief, Albanian National Liberation Army. and the negotiation of an agreement along the lines of
that concluded with the Yugoslav Government.For text of this agreement, dated March 24, 1945, see Woodbridge, UNRRA, vol. iii, p. 339. This telegram directed the insertion of a clause as follows: “Whereas the democratic government of Albania (hereinafter referred to as the government) exercises administrative authority in Albania within the meaning of Resolution I Ibid., p. 42. of the Council”.

We and our British colleagues agree that the use of the expression “democratic government of Albania” is highly undesirable and we have arranged for AFHQ to telegraph urgently to the British Military Mission at Tirana asking Brigadier HodgsonBrig. D. E. P. Hodgson, Commanding British Military Mission, Albania. to explain our position to Hendrickson and to ask him to refrain from using that terminology if in any agreement that may be negotiated. We have stated that reference to the Albanian regime should follow lines of draft prepared for military relief agreement which is: “Colonel General Enver Hoxha, Commander-in-Chief, Albania National Army of Liberation, and Principal Albanian Military Authority.” We have also emphasized that in no circumstances should the agreement imply recognition of Hoxha’s regime as government of Albania.For documentation concerning U.S. policy on recognition of a government in Albania, see vol. iv, pp. 1 ff.

In order that there may be no further hitch in this matter we hope that Department will explain to Governor Lehman that this action is in keeping with our policy and that of Great Britain with respect to question of non-recognition at least for time being of present ANLAAlbanian National Liberation Army. regime.
